report_name: AI and Multi-Channel Tactics Fuel 28% Surge in Phishing Attacks, Overwhelming Traditional Defenses

threat_actors.name: Multi-channel Phishing Actors
threat_actors.description: A general class of threat actor described in the report, characterized by the use of AI and multi-channel tactics (email, SMS, QR codes, collaboration platforms) to conduct sophisticated phishing campaigns.
threat_actors.goals: Credential theft, financial fraud (Business Email Compromise), and data breaches through large-scale, automated phishing campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The overarching tactic used in multi-channel campaigns, including email, SMS (smishing), and QR codes (quishing) to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The primary method of delivering the final phishing payload, often via email, SMS, or direct message, sometimes after a multi-stage delivery chain involving QR codes.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use services like Microsoft Teams, LinkedIn, and SMS to deliver phishing lures, bypassing email-centric security controls.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: AI-generated deepfake voice calls (vishing) are used as an emerging component of sophisticated, multi-channel social engineering campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abuse legitimate services like SharePoint and Box and use HTTPS for phishing sites to evade network filtering and signature-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: AI is used to create deepfake audio and video to impersonate trusted individuals like executives, enhancing social engineering attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: AI toolkits are used to craft highly convincing, personalized phishing lures with flawless text and to deploy AI chatbots that impersonate trusted individuals.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Attackers impersonate well-known brands, with Microsoft and Google being the most frequent targets, to exploit user trust in large-scale phishing campaigns.

tools.name: AI Phishing Toolkits
tools.description: AI toolkits, readily available on the dark web, are used to automate, scale, and personalize phishing campaigns, including generating flawless text and deepfakes.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: The most impersonated brand in phishing attacks, featured in 38% of all brand phishing attempts.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: The second most impersonated brand in phishing attacks, featured in 11% of attempts.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Hosting Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used for phishing campaigns, including newly registered domains and abused legitimate hosting services like SharePoint and Box to evade detection.

indicators.name: External User Sending Link in Microsoft Teams
indicators.pattern:
title: External User Sending Link in Microsoft Teams
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6f5e4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Monitors for external users sending messages containing URLs to internal users on Microsoft Teams. This is a tactic used in multi-channel phishing attacks.
references:
  - https://www.techradar.com/pro/phishing-attacks-surge-in-2024-as-cybercriminals-adopt-ai-tools-and-multi-channel-tactics
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-07
tags:
  - attack.t1598.003
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate collaboration with external partners.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.teams
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'MessageSent'
    UserId|contains: '#EXT#'
    MessageBody|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects when an external user sends a message containing a URL in Microsoft Teams, a technique used for multi-channel phishing.

indicators.name: Potential Payloadless BEC Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Payloadless BEC Attempt
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects emails with urgent language related to financial transactions or credential updates that do not contain common indicators of links or attachments. This is indicative of payloadless Business Email Compromise (BEC) attacks.
references:
  - https://www.techradar.com/pro/phishing-attacks-surge-in-2024-as-cybercriminals-adopt-ai-tools-and-multi-channel-tactics
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-07
tags:
  - attack.t1566
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate urgent financial or administrative requests.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    Subject|contains:
      - 'Urgent'
      - 'Request'
      - 'Payment'
    Body|contains:
      - 'wire transfer'
      - 'update credentials'
      - 'bank details'
  filter:
    Body|contains:
      - 'http'
      - 'href='
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects emails with urgent language requesting financial action or credential updates but lacking links, a common pattern in payloadless BEC attacks.

relationships.source_name: External User Sending Link in Microsoft Teams
